opportunities.title: Fix reservation SMS confirmation (RT Buckhead)
opportunities.description: The assistant collects reservation details and tells customers to check their phone for a confirmation SMS, but no SMS is ever sent. This affects 100% of RT Buckhead reservation calls.
opportunities.evidence: 39 reservation calls at RT Buckhead with 0 SMS sent. 19 calls with explicit broken promise detected.
opportunities.estimatedMonthlyImpact: 39 customers per month receive no booking confirmation. Estimated 10–20% no-show rate without confirmation.
opportunities.proposedFix: Debug and restore the SMS sender integration for the reservation confirmation flow. Add monitoring to alert if reservation SMS delivery rate drops below 90%.

opportunities.title: Eliminate avoidable transfers in TF Oakwood delivery
opportunities.description: When customers call to place a delivery or pickup order, the assistant sends them the online order link and then transfers them to staff anyway. In 93.8% of delivery calls, the transfer adds no value.
opportunities.evidence: 61 of 65 TF Oakwood delivery calls transfer after the link was sent or offered.
opportunities.estimatedMonthlyImpact: ~61 fewer staff interruptions per month. Each transfer takes ~2 minutes of staff time.
opportunities.proposedFix: Update the delivery flow: once the customer acknowledges receiving the link, end the conversation gracefully. Transfer should only happen if the customer explicitly requests it or the link fails to send.

opportunities.title: Add spam call detection and early rejection
opportunities.description: Commercial solicitation calls (Google Business account pitches, system service offers) are currently answered by the assistant before it hangs up. These calls waste API tokens and distort metrics.
opportunities.evidence: 3 spam calls detected in 5 AgentHangup events. Spam represents 60% of all AgentHangup calls.
opportunities.estimatedMonthlyImpact: Minor direct impact (~5 calls/month), but high signal value: zero legitimate calls should end in AgentHangup. Fixing this cleans up a key metric.
opportunities.proposedFix: Implement a pre-response keyword filter for the first customer message. If spam patterns are detected (commercial solicitation keywords), immediately hang up or play a rejection message.

opportunities.title: Reduce direct human-bypass rate through better assistant onboarding
opportunities.description: 29% of all calls request a human agent immediately, before attempting to use the AI assistant. This suggests customers do not know what the AI can do or do not trust it to help them.
opportunities.evidence: 87 of 300 calls (29%) have "request to speak to a human" as the primary reason.
opportunities.estimatedMonthlyImpact: If even 30% of these calls could be resolved by the AI, that is ~26 fewer transfers per month.
opportunities.proposedFix: Rewrite the opening greeting to clearly state the assistant's capabilities: 'I can help you with orders, reservations, wait times, hours, and more. How can I help you today?' Consider adding a brief 3-second pause before the greeting so callers know the call connected.

kbGaps.description: 25 delivery/pickup calls at RT Buckhead but only 4 SMS sent. The delivery flow is not configured or integrated for this restaurant.
Customers calling with app/online ordering issues are not being helped effectively. No specific flow exists for this category.

87 calls request a human immediately (TF Oakwood: 40, RT Buckhead: 47). Neither assistant has an onboarding step that demonstrates its capabilities before callers bypass to human. Adding a 5-second capability preview ("I can take orders, check hours, and make reservations — what do you need?") would reduce bypass attempts.
